---
title: "CV"
toc: false
---

Download [full CV](files/CV/Fedor_Gerasimov_CV.pdf){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}.

## Education

- **Rice University**, PhD student in Mathematics, 2023–present  
  Advisor: [Gregory Chambers](https://profiles.rice.edu/faculty/gregory-chambers){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}.  
  Research interests: discrete, convex, and computational geometry

- **Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology**, BSc in Applied Mathematics and Computer Science, 2019–2023  
  Thesis: *Blocking Numbers of Convex Bodies*, advised by [Alexander A. Polyanskii](https://polyanskii.com/){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}.

- **Yandex School of Data Analysis**, 2022–2023  
  A selective two-year graduate-level [program](https://dataschool.yandex.com/){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} in machine learning, data analysis, algorithms, and applied statistical methods.

## Publications

- Andrey S. Shutov and Fedor V. Gerasimov, [On an Interesting Circle in a Triangle](https://old.mccme.ru//circles//oim/mmks/works2019/shutgera2.pdf){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}  
Moscow Mathematical Conference of High School Students, 2019.

## Selected research experience

- **Bachelor’s thesis: Blocking Numbers of Convex Bodies**, 2022–2023  
  Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology  
  Advisor: Alexander A. Polyanskii

- **Maximum-sum matchings of points**, Summer 2021  
  Undergraduate research project at MIPT  
  Advisor: Alexander A. Polyanskii

- **Rigidity of Laman graphs and the Bellows conjecture**, 2020–2021  
  Undergraduate research practicum at MIPT  
  Advisor: Alexander A. Polyanskii

## Teaching experience

- **Mathematics Instructor**, Tapia Camp at Rice University, Summer 2025  
  Instructor for *The Techniques of a Pro Mathematician* for high-school students.

- **Teaching Assistant**, Rice University, Spring 2025  
  CMOR 360: *Introduction to Optimization and Operations Research*.

- **Olympiad Mathematics Coach**, Lyceum “The Second School,” 2020–2021  
  Organized a curriculum in advanced olympiad mathematics for 8th-grade students.

## Selected awards

- **All-Russian National Olympiad in Mathematics**, Silver Medal, 2019
- **Moscow Mathematical Conference of High School Students**, Best Project Award, 2020
- **Higher School of Economics Mathematical Olympiad**, Gold Medals, 2018 and 2019
- **Moscow Mathematical Olympiad**, Silver Medals, 2018 and 2019

## Conferences and programs

- **SIAM TX/LA Conference**, 2024  
  Presented the poster *Exploring Local Solution Manifolds of Nonlinear Least Squares Problems*.

- **International Conference on the Geometry and Topology of 3-Manifolds**, 2022

- **Workshop on the Contemporary Problems of Applied Combinatorics**, 2021  
  Gave a talk on modeling an epidemic on a random graph.

## Skills

**Programming:** Python, C++, R, MATLAB, Wolfram Mathematica, Lean
**Tools:** LaTeX, Markdown, Gurobi  
**Languages:** Russian (mother tongue), English, Spanish (novice)
